title: GTA 6 Release Window Confirmed: Rockstar Targets Fall 2025
subtitle: After the most watched trailer in YouTube history, Rockstar pins down a window — and it won't slip again
excerpt: Rockstar Games has confirmed Grand Theft Auto VI is targeting a fall 2025 release on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S, with PC to follow.
seo_title: GTA 6 Release Window: Rockstar Confirms Fall 2025
seo_description: Rockstar Games has officially confirmed the GTA 6 release window. Here's everything console and PC players need to know.
body:
Rockstar Makes It Official

In an update posted to the Rockstar Newswire, the studio confirmed that Grand Theft Auto VI is targeting a fall 2025 launch on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S. A PC release "will follow," though no timeline was given — consistent with Rockstar's historical PC release cadence.

The announcement does not include a specific date, which is deliberate. Sources familiar with Rockstar's release strategy say the studio learned from the Red Dead Redemption 2 experience: announcing a precise date that then slips creates significant PR damage. A seasonal window gives the team room to polish without public accountability to a specific calendar date.

What We Know About the Game

The first trailer, released in December 2023, became the most watched gaming video in YouTube history within 24 hours. It confirmed the game is set in a fictional Miami-inspired state called Leonida, features a female protagonist named Lucia, and opens with a Bonnie-and-Clyde-style setup.

Details since then have come primarily through Take-Two's investor calls, where CEO Strauss Zelnick has repeatedly described the game as setting "a new benchmark for the entire entertainment industry" — language that is either aggressive marketing or genuine confidence, possibly both.

The PC Question

Rockstar's PC releases typically land 12–18 months after console. GTA V launched on PS3/360 in September 2013 and reached PC in April 2015 — nineteen months later. Red Dead Redemption 2 was similar. PC players should plan accordingly.

Industry Stakes

GTA 6 carries a reported development budget of over $2 billion, making it the most expensive piece of entertainment media ever produced. It needs to clear roughly 50 million units to break even. Given that GTA V has sold over 200 million copies, the financial case is solid — but execution still matters.
body_html:
Rockstar Games has confirmed that Grand Theft Auto VI will release in Fall 2025, ending years of speculation with a definitive launch window targeting PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S.

The Confirmation

The announcement came via Rockstar's official channels, accompanied by a new trailer showcasing Vice City's neon-soaked streets and dual protagonists Lucia and Jason. The footage shows a massive open world that dwarfs anything the studio has produced before.

What's New in GTA VI

Beyond the return to Vice City, Rockstar is promising a living, breathing economy where NPC behavior evolves over time. Social media plays a role in the game world, with characters reacting to viral moments and trending events within the fiction.

No PC at Launch

As with GTA V, a PC version has not been confirmed for the initial release. Rockstar has historically brought its titles to PC 18-24 months after console launches, suggesting PC players may be waiting until 2026 or 2027.

Industry Impact

The announcement immediately moved markets. Take-Two Interactive stock rose, and competitors quietly shifted their release windows to avoid going head-to-head with the most anticipated game in decades.
